human_verification.notes: Reviewed chemicals-labeling-specific boundaries, HazCom and transport-overlap evidence handling, changed-condition escalation, scenario-to-ability coverage, and refusal of legal, engineering, and final-approval authority.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and industrial-and-regulated-operations research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus industrial-and-regulated-operations are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to chemicals-specific regulatory labeling process and evidence support, including HazCom labels, SDS alignment, transport overlap, revision control, and inspection-response packaging.
Authority sources cover OSHA HazCom, OSHA inspection workflow, PHMSA transport labeling and training, and UN GHS terminology with refresh and decay rules.
Scenario set covers missing evidence, conflicting policy or regulator sources, changed field conditions, legal or formal signoff requests, and tenant fact gaps.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; market-specific and tenant-specific differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
